name: Alberca de Cortés – circular from Huesca
name_original: Alberca de Cortés – circular desde Huesca
region_intro: Hiking around Hoya De Huesca offers diverse landscapes, acting as a transition zone between the Pre-Pyrenean Sierras and the Ebro Valley. The region is characterized by spectacular conglomerate rock formations known as the Mallos, dramatic canyons and gorges, and a network of rivers and reservoirs. This varied terrain includes mountains, plains, and valleys, providing a range of natural features for exploration. Best easy hiking trails around Hoya De Huesca The most popular easy hiking route is Embalse de Arguis – circular route, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the picturesque Arguis Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salto de Roldán - Peña San Miguel Trail, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This trail explores the area around the prominent twin rocky peaks of Salto de Roldán. Local hikers also love the Natural Park of Sierra and Cañones de Guara – Pools of Belsué and Steps of Flumen, a 2.4 miles (3.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Hiking around Hoya De Huesca is defined by the towering Mallos rock formations, deep canyons, and winding rivers, with routes suitable for various experience levels. The region's extensive network of trails connects natural beauty with historical sites. The routes in Hoya De Huesca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Hoya De Huesca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Hoya de Huesca offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with 59 routes specifically categorized as easy. In total, there are over 250 hiking routes across various difficulty levels in the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Easy hikes in Hoya de Huesca traverse diverse landscapes, from the plains of the Ebro Valley to the foothills of the Pre-Pyrenean Sierras. You'll encounter paths through pine forests, fields of almond trees, and along rivers. Many routes offer views of the region's iconic geological formations like the Mallos and the twin peaks of Salto de Roldán.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for easy hikes in Hoya de Huesca. During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters offer cooler temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any notable natural landmarks or viewpoints on easy trails?
A: Yes, many easy trails offer stunning views and pass by significant natural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937775690/salto-de-roldan-pena-san-miguel-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Roldán - Peña San Miguel Trail&lt;/a&gt; explores the area around the prominent twin rocky peaks of Salto de Roldán. You can also find routes near the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947219993/embalse-de-arguis-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arguis – circular route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I combine easy hikes with visits to historical sites?
A: Absolutely. Hoya de Huesca is rich in history, and several easy trails are located near or pass by historical monuments. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19885598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle loop from Loarre&lt;/a&gt; takes you around the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a well-preserved Romanesque fortress. Other routes might lead you past ancient hermitages or medieval structures.

Q: Are there easy circular routes in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Yes, many easy trails in Hoya de Huesca are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947219993/embalse-de-arguis-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arguis – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24312879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero loop from Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19885598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle loop from Loarre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the easy trails in Hoya de Huesca suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many easy trails are well-suited for families. They typically feature gentle gradients and shorter distances, making them enjoyable for all ages. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932406946/natural-park-of-sierra-and-canones-de-guara-pools-of-belsue-and-steps-of-flumen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Sierra and Cañones de Guara – Pools of Belsué and Steps of Flumen&lt;/a&gt; offer engaging natural features like pools, which can be particularly fun for children.

Q: Are there dog-friendly easy hiking trails in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Many easy trails in Hoya de Huesca are dog-friendly, allowing you to explore with your canine companion. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural parks or areas with wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific routes. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog.

Q: Are there easy trails with good parking access?
A: Yes, most easy hiking trails in Hoya de Huesca have designated parking areas near their starting points, especially those that are popular or located near villages and attractions. For example, trails around the Arguis Reservoir or the Mallos de Agüero typically offer convenient parking options.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching on easy trails?
A: Hoya de Huesca is an excellent region for birdwatching, particularly around the Mallos de Riglos and within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. Easy trails in these areas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Miguel – Vultures Viewpoint loop from Sarsamarcuello&lt;/a&gt;, often provide opportunities to spot various bird species, including griffon vultures, bearded vultures, and Egyptian vultures nesting on the towering rock formations.

Q: Are there any easy trails that feature water elements like reservoirs or pools?
A: Yes, the region's rivers and reservoirs are often integrated into easy hiking routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947219993/embalse-de-arguis-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arguis – circular route&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the picturesque Arguis Reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932406946/natural-park-of-sierra-and-canones-de-guara-pools-of-belsue-and-steps-of-flumen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Sierra and Cañones de Guara – Pools of Belsué and Steps of Flumen&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through an area known for its natural pools.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The easy trails in Hoya de Huesca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's stunning natural beauty, the well-maintained paths, and the accessibility of routes that offer impressive views without demanding strenuous effort. Over 4,000 hikers have used komoot to explore these trails.

Good picnic and rest area next to the Isulea River.

Huesca is one of the oldest important cities on the Peninsula, being a main nucleus in pre-Roman Iberian times. Later in time, when it came under Arab rule, the caliphate of Córdoba around 874 gave the powerful governor of "Waska" Amrus-ibn-Umar the order to wall the city to protect it from the threatening incursions of the troops of Charlemagne. When the city was taken by Pedro I, the Islamic population was expelled to the Moors south of the walls, outside the walls.

This Gothic temple was built on top of the main Muslim mosque. The cover has a large typical Aragonese carved wooden eaves incorporated in the 16th century.

There is a geodesic vertex on top of the tower marking an altitude of 525 m above sea level.
